title: Neural Interface Milestone: May 2026
author: Anonymous Researcher
Chronicles Archive
description: 10,000 channel brain-computer interface working. Are we becoming cyborgs? First successful high-bandwidth neural recordings. The line between human and machine getting blurry.
content:
**May 22, 2026**

Interesting lunch convo: "How many neurons do you need to interface with before you're technically a cyborg?"

Asking for a friend who may or may not have a device in the next room with 10,000 channels.

(The friend is me. The device is [REDACTED].)

---

**June 2, 2026**

Successfully recorded from 10,000 neurons simultaneously today. Clean signal. Real-time processing. Bidirectional communication working.

We can read thoughts. Not perfectly, but... we can read thoughts.

The ethics committee is going to have a field day with this.

---

**June 30, 2026**

End of Q2 demo day. What I saw today:
- AI that [REDACTED]
- Quantum system that [REDACTED]
- Nanotech that [REDACTED]

Can't share any of it. But trust me: The 2030s are going to be WILD.

---

The brain-computer interface worked. Too well.

We could read neural signals. Soon we'd be able to write them too.

— *Recovered from personal archive, 2030*

**Related Chronicles:**
- [Neural Implant Rejection (2030)](/articles/neural-implant-rejection-2030)
- [Human-AI Merger Identity Crisis (2057)](/articles/human-ai-merger-identity-crisis-2057)
